what_is_open: Determine the exact welfare-regret exponent for genuinely feasible estimation in the strict-gap branch r_feas(alpha,gamma,a,c)<r_star(alpha,gamma), especially under weak overlap gamma>0. Equivalently, prove either an improved feasible achievability result at exponent r_star(alpha,gamma), or a sharper information-theoretic lower bound showing that every feasible procedure is slower because weak-arm outcome regression must be learned on the clipped region.
obstruction: The available DAG supplies only two nonmatching facts in this branch. First, thm:minimax-lower gives M_n(alpha,gamma)>=c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} over all measurable Pi-valued estimators; this lower bound is too weak to force the slower exponent r_feas when r_feas<r_star. Second, oeq:feasible-upper gives one explicit feasible cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM with risk at most C n^{-r_feas}(log n)^p; this upper bound may reflect either an intrinsic weak-arm nuisance-estimation cost or a limitation of the clipped-AIPW analysis. The frozen core does not define a restricted estimator class whose information lower bound captures 'genuinely feasible' learning of the weak-arm outcome regression, nor does it supply an alternative construction removing the additive clipped-region drift.
attempted: Using thm:rate-characterization closes the question whenever r_feas=r_star, as proved in lem:feasible-attainment-at-equal-exponents. In the strict-gap branch it yields only the bracket proved in lem:strict-gap-bracket. A slower feasible lower bound cannot be inferred from the current minimax converse because M_n already infimizes over all measurable estimators and n^{-r_star} is a smaller risk order than n^{-r_feas}. Conversely, r_star-achievability cannot be inferred for the plain clipped-AIPW ERM because its proved risk decomposition contains the global additive drift r_mu,n r_e,n/q + r_mu,n q^{1/(2 gamma)}, and no current lemma shows that this drift can be localized away or orthogonalized under the same assumptions.
partial_result: Exact feasible tightness is proved up to logarithms on the equal-exponent branch r_feas(alpha,gamma,a,c)=r_star(alpha,gamma): the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM attains n^{-r_star}, and thm:minimax-lower rules out any faster polynomial exponent. At gamma=0 this requires and is equivalent to c>=A_alpha=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). In the remaining strict-gap branch the strongest proved statement is c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma,a,c)}(log n)^p with r_feas<r_star; whether this bracket is tight remains the isolated open problem.
